title: World Cup Confederation Funnel — 1982–2026
description: Every FIFA World Cup from 1982 to 2026 as a confederation funnel: FIFA members -> direct berths -> inter-confederation play-offs -> qualified field -> knockout rounds -> champion, phases aligned by teams remaining.
liveNote: 2026 is in progress; the Round of 16 is under way (as of 5 July 2026). Morocco (3-0 v Canada) and France (1-0 v Paraguay) are the first two quarter-finalists, so the quarter-finals are shown as a partial tier (see 'partialTiers', 2 of 8 so far); tiers at/after each edition's 'pendingFrom' key (sf onward for 2026) are not yet played.

qualificationNotes.summary: No inter-confederation play-offs. Asia and Oceania shared one combined qualifying zone allocated two berths (taken by Kuwait and New Zealand).
UEFA v OFC play-off: Scotland beat Australia, so the final European berth went to UEFA and Oceania sent no team. Mexico replaced Colombia as host.
CONMEBOL v OFC play-off: Colombia beat Israel (then competing in the Oceania zone), so South America took the berth and Oceania sent no team.
CONMEBOL v OFC play-off: Argentina beat Australia. Reunified Germany competed for the first time (auto-qualified as holders); the USSR had dissolved, so Russia debuted as an independent nation.
First 32-team World Cup. AFC v OFC play-off: Iran beat Australia on away goals, so Asia took the berth and Oceania again sent no team. Host France and holders Brazil both auto-qualified.

Co-hosts South Korea and Japan (both AFC) auto-qualified. UEFA v AFC play-off: Rep. of Ireland beat Iran. CONMEBOL v OFC play-off: Uruguay beat Australia. Last edition with an automatic holders’ berth (France).

AFC v CONCACAF play-off: Trinidad & Tobago beat Bahrain. OFC v CONMEBOL play-off: Australia beat Uruguay — Australia is credited to OFC here (its move to the AFC took effect 1 Jan 2006, after qualification). Holders’ auto-berth abolished from this edition.

First African-hosted World Cup; host South Africa was the first host ever eliminated in the group stage. CONCACAF v CONMEBOL play-off: Uruguay beat Costa Rica. AFC v OFC play-off: New Zealand beat Bahrain. Australia now attributed to the AFC.
Host Brazil (CONMEBOL) auto-qualified. CONMEBOL v AFC play-off: Uruguay beat Jordan. CONCACAF v OFC play-off: Mexico beat New Zealand.
Host Russia (UEFA) auto-qualified. AFC v CONCACAF play-off: Australia beat Honduras. CONMEBOL v OFC play-off: Peru beat New Zealand. All four semi-finalists were European.

Host Qatar (AFC) auto-qualified — the first host to lose all three group games. AFC–CONMEBOL play-off: Australia beat Peru. CONCACAF–OFC play-off: Costa Rica beat New Zealand. Morocco became the first African (and Arab) semi-finalist.

First 48-team World Cup, with three CONCACAF hosts (all auto-qualified) and a new Round of 32. A six-team intercontinental play-off (March 2026) awarded two places: DR Congo (CAF) and Iraq (AFC) won them — so the two extra berths went outside their pattern, lifting CAF to 10 and AFC to 9. OFC received its first-ever guaranteed direct slot (New Zealand).

methodology: Teams are attributed to the confederation they qualified THROUGH in that cycle (e.g. Australia = OFC for 2006, having qualified via Oceania before its 1 Jan 2006 move; AFC from 2010).

directBerths are the guaranteed slots, including hosts and (through 2002) the defending champions. Inter-confederation play-offs award the remaining places, credited as +1 to the winning confederation; directBerths + play-off wins reconcile exactly to fieldSize.
playoffs[].winner is the confederation that won each contested place; it always equals the set of confederations where qualified-count minus directBerths == 1.

FIFA-membership figures (fifaMembers) for past editions are approximate reconstructions from confederation admission dates; only the 2026 breakdown (membersExact:true; UEFA 55, CAF 54, AFC 46, CONCACAF 35, CONMEBOL 10, OFC 11 = 211) is exact.
1982 used a two-group-stage format (24 -> 12 -> 4 semi-finalists), so it has 'r12' (second round) instead of a Round of 16 / quarter-finals.
2026 introduces a Round of 32 (48 -> 32). Phases align by teams remaining, so 2026's r32 is comparable to the 32-team fields of 1998-2022.
sources: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1982_FIFA_World_Cup (and the 1986...2026 tournament, _qualification and _knockout_stage pages for every edition)
Live 2026 results cross-checked against ESPN, BBC Sport, CBS Sports & FIFA.com (accessed 4 July 2026)
